summary: fitScorer's keyword-extraction pulls non-skill garbage tokens (raw URLs, anti-scrape honeypot codes, concatenated words) out of live job descriptions and surfaces them as the headline 'Skill gap' in the AI Match Analysis card shown to the paying user.
reproduction: Log in as admin/admin123 (Pro entitlement active).
Navigate to /dashboard/jobs.
Select job id ccf9af5cfbc634f860c727c95 ('Project Manager' @ Empire Life, source=remoteok) or call GET /jobs/{id}/insights directly.
Read the 'AI Match Analysis' card's 'Skill gap' value / the API response's skillGap field.
Observe skillGap = 'rmja4ljeymi44ljex' -- traced to an anti-AI-scraper honeypot phrase embedded in the live posting text ('tag RMjA4LjEyMi44LjEx when applying').
Repeat for job id c3a9c67fb2da7ec7c416a8a06 (skillGap='cdn.openai.com', a URL from the raw description) and cb4f11f939445b2a2e383a6af (skillGap='accommodations', a stray word).
expected: The 'Skill gap' shown to a paying user should be a plausible, human-readable skill/technology, not a URL fragment, an anti-scraper honeypot code, or a stray non-skill word pulled verbatim from unstructured description text.
observed: skillGap and matchedSkills/missingSkills arrays regularly contain non-skill noise tokens extracted from raw description text, shown verbatim in the primary AI Match Analysis card.
evidence: curl GET /jobs/ccf9af5cfbc634f860c727c95/insights and GET /jobs/ccf9af5cfbc634f860c727c95 (description text) -- captured 2026-07-17T15:44Z and re-confirmed 2026-07-17T15:58Z (stable/deterministic, reproduced twice)

summary: Bulk 'Apply (N)' applies directly to every selected job with NO resume tailoring step and NO confirmation gate, unlike the wireframe's 'Tailor & Apply (4)' bulk button and unlike the single-job flow, which requires Tailor Resume -> Review & Apply -> an explicit 'this action cannot be undone' confirmation modal before submitting.
reproduction: Log in, go to /dashboard/jobs, Australia tab.
Tick the checkbox on one not-yet-applied job card (e.g. 'Senior Product Manager, Strategic Origination Platforms' @ Plenti).
Observe the bulk action button reads 'Apply (1)' (not 'Tailor & Apply (1)').
Click 'Apply (1)'.

Observe: no confirmation dialog appears, no tailoring progress is shown; the job immediately flips to status=applied (confirmed via GET /jobs) and an Application record is created with status=submitted.
expected: Per the wireframe (btn-bulk-tailor-jd10, label 'Tailor & Apply (4)') and per the single-job apply flow's own explicit confirmation gate ('This action cannot be undone'), a bulk apply action should either tailor each resume first or, at minimum, show the same irreversible-action confirmation gate the single-job flow enforces.
observed: bulkApply() in apps/web/src/app/dashboard/jobs/page.tsx calls POST /jobs/{id}/apply directly for every selected id, in a loop, with no tailor call and no confirmation modal.
evidence: GET /jobs response for ce2f682aeba72f9e9b07ff083 showing status=applied, updatedAt=2026-07-17T15:55:29Z immediately after the single click, captured 2026-07-17T15:55Z

summary: The 'Connected Job Boards' source integration bar structurally diverges from the wireframe: the wireframe (jd22-jd28) shows named consumer job boards (Seek, LinkedIn AU, Workforce AU, Jora, Indeed AU) each with a per-source connection-status dot and a 'Connect via Browser' / 'Manage' / 'Connect via MyGov' action button; the live implementation instead renders one card per ACTUAL backend data source (Greenhouse, ashby, Lever, RemoteOK, Remotive, ...) showing only a live job count, with no per-source connect/manage affordance and no red/yellow/green connection-state semantics matching the wireframe.
reproduction: Log in, go to /dashboard/jobs.
Compare the 'CONNECTED JOB BOARDS' section against design/screens/job-discovery.html lines 63-122 (source-bar-jd22).
expected: Visual/structural conformance to the wireframe's per-source connect/manage card pattern.
observed: Cards show only source name + live job count; no connect/manage buttons; Seek/LinkedIn AU/Workforce AU/Jora/Indeed AU (as named in the wireframe) are entirely absent, replaced by the real ATS-aggregator sources actually wired up.

summary: The wireframe's 'Role' (filter-role-jd04) and 'Salary' (filter-salary-jd06) filter dropdowns have no equivalent in the live implementation -- only Source, Location (text), Remote toggle, Sort, and Match-min slider are present.
reproduction: Log in, go to /dashboard/jobs.
Inspect the filter bar (data-testid=job-filter-bar).
Compare against design/screens/job-discovery.html lines 125-133 which shows Role, Source, Location, Salary, Remote-Hybrid, Match>= controls.
expected: A Role filter and a Salary filter, per the approved wireframe.
observed: Only 5 controls present: job-source-filter (select), job-location-filter (text input), remote-toggle, a sort-by select, and match-min-slider. No role or salary filtering is possible anywhere on the screen.

summary: Tailor-resume runs frequently apply 0 (or 1) of ~8 LLM-proposed bullet edits because the fabrication guard rejects the rest, with no user-facing explanation of why -- the UI just shows 'N rejected by fabrication guard' with no detail, after a ~90-100s wait and a real LLM spend.
reproduction: Select a job, click 'Tailor Resume ->'.
Wait ~90s for step 2 to appear.
Read 'X changes applied ... Y rejected by fabrication guard'.
Observed twice on the same job: run 1 = 1 applied / 7 rejected; run 2 (re-tailor) = 0 applied / 8 rejected.
expected: Either a higher accept rate, or an explanation/preview of what was rejected and why, so a paying user understands the value received for the LLM spend and wait.
observed: Binary 'N rejected by fabrication guard' with no elaboration; two consecutive runs on the same job yielded 1/8 and 0/8 accepted changes respectively.
evidence: GET /agents/runs entries c3110b283d4e6ed04a22d5f9b (changes=1) and c89e9ae179c6b2ccd500141e4 (changes=0), both job_id=c79e53e764c3a08088d24dde7
